county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sac County formed 1851 from Greene. County seat is Sac City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Sac City holds deeds from 1856; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Sac County was named for the Sauk (Sac) people. Settled in the 1860s-1870s. German and Scandinavian immigrants. Sac City's world's largest popcorn ball. Sac County Historical Society. Lake View, Schaller, and Early communities. North Raccoon River. Black Hawk Lake in Lake View is Iowa's only natural lake formed by glacial action in the western part of the state. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Sac County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Sac County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Sac City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1851 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Sac County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Sac City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Sac County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Sac City / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Sac County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Sac County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Sac County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Sac County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Sac County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Sac County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Sac County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Sac County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Sac County Auditor, Sac City
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Sac County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Scott County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Davenport.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Davenport holds deeds from 1838; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Scott County is Iowa's third most populous county, centered on Davenport (part of the Quad Cities). The German immigrant community is exceptionally strong -- Turner societies, German-language newspapers, and German churches. Putnam Museum and Science Center has local history collections. Richardson-Sloane Special Collections Center at Davenport Public Library. Scott County Historical Society. Palmer College of Chiropractic (founded 1897) records. Rock Island Arsenal across the river. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Scott County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.repository: State Historical Society of Iowa / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Scott County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, land sale notices. State Historical Society of Iowa holds microfilm of many Iowa newspapers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Shelby County formed 1851 from Cass. County seat is Harlan.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Harlan holds deeds from 1853; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Shelby County was settled by Danish and German immigrants in the 1860s-1870s. The Elk Horn-Kimballton Danish settlement extends into Shelby County from Audubon County. Danish Lutheran church records. Shelby County Historical Society. Defiance, Earling, and Panama communities. West Nishnabotna River. German Catholic parishes in Earling and Portsmouth. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Shelby County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sioux County formed 1851 from Indian lands. County seat is Orange City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Orange City holds deeds from 1870; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Sioux County is the most strongly Dutch county in Iowa. Orange City was founded by Henry Hospers and Dutch colonists. Northwestern College (est. 1882) archives. Dutch Reformed church records dominate -- they are essential for nearly all early families. Sioux County Historical Society. Alton, Boyden, Hawarden, Hull, and Rock Valley communities. Sioux Center and Dordt University (est. 1955) archives. Big Sioux and Floyd Rivers. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Sioux County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Story County formed 1846 from Boone, Jasper. County seat is Nevada.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Nevada holds deeds from 1854; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Story County is home to Ames and Iowa State University (ISU, est. 1858 as Iowa Agricultural College). ISU Library Special Collections hold extensive agricultural, engineering, and regional manuscript collections. Story County Historical Society. Nevada (the county seat) and Ames developed as railroad communities. Cambridge, Huxley (Norwegian heritage), and Roland communities. Skunk River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Story County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Tama County formed 1843 from Benton, Black Hawk. County seat is Toledo.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Toledo holds deeds from 1853; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Tama County is uniquely significant because of the Meskwaki (Sac and Fox) Settlement near Tama -- the only federally recognized tribal settlement in Iowa, established when the Meskwaki purchased land in 1857 (they were not placed on a reservation by the government). Meskwaki tribal records, BIA records at NARA (Kansas City), and Iowa State Archives hold related materials. Tama County Historical Society. Czech settlers in Clutier and Vining. Lincoln Highway passes through. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Tama County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1843. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1843. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Meskwaki (Sac and Fox) Settlement Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA Kansas City / Bureau of Indian Affairs / Iowa State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Records of the Meskwaki (Sac and Fox) Settlement near Tama, the only tribally purchased settlement in Iowa. BIA agency records, tribal census rolls, allotment records, and school records. Meskwaki tribal government may have additional records with restricted access.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BIA records at NARA are substantially complete; tribal records may have access restrictions

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Taylor County formed 1847 from Page. County seat is Bedford.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Bedford holds deeds from 1851; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Taylor County in southwestern Iowa was settled by migrants from Missouri and the upper South. Bedford was an agricultural market town. Taylor County Historical Society. Lenox and New Market communities. One Hundred and Two River valley (Platte River tributary). Gravity and Conway communities. BLM GLO patents online. FamilySearch microfilm.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Taylor County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Union County formed 1851 from Clarke. County seat is Creston.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Creston holds deeds from 1855; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Union County was a Burlington and Missouri River Railroad (later CB&Q) division point at Creston. Railroad employment records document many worker families. Creston was also a wind energy pioneer. Union County Historical Society. Afton and Lorimor communities. Twelve Mile Creek. Mormon Trail passed through the area. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Union County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Van Buren County formed 1836 from Des Moines. County seat is Keosauqua.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Keosauqua holds deeds from 1837; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Van Buren County is one of Iowa's earliest counties with some of the state's oldest structures. Bentonsport, Bonaparte, and Keosauqua are historic villages along the Des Moines River with well-preserved 19th-century architecture. Van Buren County Historical Society. Lacey-Keosauqua State Park. Settlers from Pennsylvania, Virginia, and Kentucky. Iowaville cemetery (Chief Wapello burial site). FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Van Buren County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Keosauqua
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1836 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Keosauqua
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1836. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Van Buren County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Keosauqua / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1836. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Van Buren County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Van Buren County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Van Buren County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Van Buren County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Van Buren County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Van Buren County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Van Buren County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Van Buren County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Van Buren County Auditor, Keosauqua
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Van Buren County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Van Buren County Mining Records and Mine Inspector Reports
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / State Historical Society of Iowa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mine inspector reports, coal company records, mine accident reports, and related court records for Van Buren County coal mining communities. Iowa Mine Inspector annual reports list mines, operators, and sometimes workers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wapello County formed 1843 from Indian Cession. County seat is Ottumwa.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Ottumwa holds deeds from 1844; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Wapello County is centered on Ottumwa, a major meatpacking and industrial city (John Morrell & Co.). Morrell employment records document diverse worker communities. Ottumwa was the birthplace of Radar O'Reilly (M*A*S*H). Wapello County Historical Society. Coal mining heritage in south Wapello County. Des Moines River valley. Chillicothe, Agency, and Blakesburg communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Wapello County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Wapello County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Ottumwa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1843 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Wapello County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Ottumwa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1843. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Wapello County Historic Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: State Historical Society of Iowa / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
